You are a project manager for the Code Review Platform project.

A developer has raised this issue: "Implement incremental diff engine to show only changed lines in large PRs"
Domain: developer-tools | Complexity: high

Break this down into a well-structured task with full project management detail.

Return JSON with this exact structure:
{
  "task_id": "TASK-XXX",
  "title": "short action-verb title",
  "description": "2-3 sentence detailed description",
  "acceptance_criteria": ["criterion 1", "criterion 2", "criterion 3"],
  "story_points": <fibonacci: 1,2,3,5,8,13>,
  "priority": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
  "labels": ["label1", "label2"],
  "dependencies": ["describe any blocking tasks or leave empty array"],
  "definition_of_done": "clear measurable done state"
}

{
  "task_id": "TASK-427",
  "title": "Implement Incremental Diff Engine",
  "description": "This task involves developing and integrating an incremental diff engine into the code review platform. The engine will efficiently identify and display only the changed lines within large pull requests, improving review performance and reducing cognitive load for reviewers. This will significantly enhance the user experience when dealing with substantial code changes.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "The diff engine accurately identifies and displays only the changed lines in pull requests exceeding 500 lines of code.",
    "The incremental diff view loads and renders within 5 seconds for pull requests up to 1000 lines of code.",
    "The incremental diff view is visually consistent with the existing full diff view and provides clear highlighting of added and removed lines."
  ],
  "story_points": 8,
  "priority": "High",
  "labels": [
    "performance",
    "code-review",
    "diff",
    "developer-tools"
  ],
  "dependencies": [
    "TASK-426: Investigate and select appropriate diff algorithm library"
  ],
  "definition_of_done": "Code is merged into the main branch, unit and integration tests pass with 100% coverage, performance benchmarks meet acceptance criteria, and the feature is documented in the user guide."
}
